reachability_artifacts: TestOllamaVerify_ReturnsContent (unit): Verify() calls /api/chat and extracts message.content

design_decisions.summary: No OllamaConfig struct — two constructor params (modelID, host) with empty-host → $OLLAMA_HOST fallback inside NewOllama, following spec literally.
design_decisions.rationale: One parameter fewer than the provider-config pattern; Ollama is local/free, no auth/region/endpoint complexity.

design_decisions.summary: OllamaHost field stays raw host (no /v1); the change is removing the /v1 append in the dispatch block, not changing the stored value.
design_decisions.rationale: ollamaHost() already returns the raw host — field semantics unchanged; only the consumer's URL construction changes.

design_decisions.summary: Reuse ollamaHost() in NewOllama for empty-host param; the $OLLAMA_HOST fallback handles direct construction (tests, standalone) — NewClient always passes pcfg.OllamaHost.
design_decisions.rationale: Spec requires NewOllama("m", "") to resolve $OLLAMA_HOST; NewClient path supplies a non-empty host from ProviderConfigFromEnv.

design_decisions.summary: JSON decode into anonymous struct with only needed fields (message.content, error, done); unknown fields silently ignored — same pattern as OAI driver.
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with existing driver patterns; stdlib encoding/json handles unknown field passthrough.

design_decisions.summary: Error on "error" response field uses fmt.Errorf with the error string — not *model.Error taxonomy, because Ollama is local/free with no auth/rate-limit/billing states.
design_decisions.rationale: Ollama has no rate limits, auth errors, or billing states to classify; a plain error string matches the local-tool posture.
